# Cortex Finance Monitor

> Real-time finance and trading dashboard tracking global markets, stock exchanges, central banks, commodities, forex, crypto, and economic indicators in one view.

Cortex Finance Monitor is an open-source (AGPL-3.0) finance dashboard. It is a rebranded deployment of the finance variant of the open-source World Monitor platform by Elie Habib, with live data feeds (markets, macro, commodities, prediction markets) and an AI market analyst. It runs as a web app and installable PWA.

The project is built with TypeScript, Vite, MapLibre GL JS, deck.gl, and D3.js. Browser-side clustering and local analysis run in the client; serverless APIs publish cached market, macro, and forecast data refreshed by a scheduled data pipeline.

## Main pages

- [Dashboard](https://cortex-finance-monitor.vercel.app): Live markets, central banks, commodities, forex, crypto, economic indicators
- [Pro](https://cortex-finance-monitor.vercel.app/pro): AI analyst, personalized digests, custom widgets, MCP for Claude & GPT

## Documentation

- [Source Code](https://github.com/pilotnavia/cortex-finance-monitor): GitHub repository (AGPL-3.0)
- [README](https://github.com/pilotnavia/cortex-finance-monitor/blob/main/README.md): Project documentation
- [Onboarding Guide](https://github.com/pilotnavia/cortex-finance-monitor/blob/main/docs/CORTEX-ONBOARDING.md): Architecture, variant system, Pro model, data pipeline
- [Extended LLM Documentation](https://cortex-finance-monitor.vercel.app/llms-full.txt): Comprehensive version of this file

## Key Features

- Live market quotes, sector heatmaps, and market sentiment indicators
- Macro and central bank data (FRED, IMF, BIS) and commodity/energy data (EIA)
- Prediction markets (Polymarket, Kalshi) and AI-generated market forecasts
- Interactive map with financial centers, stock exchanges, and central banks
- AI market analyst (Pro) with live data context
- 21+ language support with RTL

## Attribution

- [World Monitor](https://github.com/koala73/worldmonitor): The upstream open-source platform this project is based on (AGPL-3.0, by Elie Habib)
